metadata.title: nonansNRI: Numerical Runtime Intelligence (NRI) v0.4.0 — reference implementation
metadata.description:
Reference implementation of the Numerical Runtime Intelligence (NRI) framework (project: nonansNRI; commercial entity: nonans): a runtime instrumentation layer for the real-time classification of legitimate numerical events (overflow, structural rank collapse, attention concentration) in transformer training and inference.

Includes the three-gate monitoring engine, ghost/real fault classifier, FaultContext interface, six deterministic benchmarks (seed 20260525), four PyTorch reproducibility protocols, and the NumPy algebraic validation suite. Reviewer-facing reproducibility test reproduces the central mathematical claim (signal_score(sv) ≡ attention_entropy_normalized(sv/sum(sv)) to max error 3.18×10⁻⁷ over N=10,000 samples) in under five minutes on a laptop with only numpy installed.

The proprietary resolution layer at the runtime boundary is maintained in a separate private codebase and is not included in this deposit.
metadata.notes: Independent research project. All six benchmarks reproduce bit-for-bit on IEEE-754 hardware with seed 20260525. GPU overhead and real-model lead-time validation are explicitly outstanding; protocols included in the deposit are self-validating and ready to run on accelerator hardware. The paper accompanying this software deposit is licensed CC BY 4.0; the code is licensed Apache-2.0; the proprietary resolution layer is not deposited.
